_note: Only allergy-based restrictions from official labeling and package inserts.

allergies.reaction: Anaphylaxis, urticaria, angioedema, bronchospasm, anaphylactic shock
allergies.cross_reactivity_note: Approximately 1-10% cross-reactivity with cephalosporins. Avoid all penicillin-class antibiotics in confirmed penicillin anaphylaxis.
Cross-reactivity with penicillins possible. Take allergy history carefully.
Cross-reactivity within fluoroquinolone class likely. Avoid all fluoroquinolones if hypersensitivity documented.

allergies.reaction: Urticaria, angioedema, bronchospasm, anaphylaxis, Samter's triad (aspirin-exacerbated respiratory disease)
allergies.cross_reactivity_note: Patients with aspirin hypersensitivity may react to other NSAIDs. Aspirin-exacerbated respiratory disease common in asthmatic patients.
Cross-reactivity within macrolide class (clarithromycin, erythromycin). Document allergy clearly.

allergies.reaction: Anaphylaxis, angioedema, serious skin reactions (SJS, TEN, DRESS)
allergies.cross_reactivity_note: Cross-reactivity possible across PPI class (omeprazole, lansoprazole, rabeprazole).
True paracetamol allergy is rare but documented. Cross-reactivity with NSAIDs not expected.
